title: Surfaces, Draw Order, Alpha, Transforms, Assets, and Presentation · Failure Triage
summary: A preserved game trace contains one plausible state, timing, input, vector, rendering, collision, ownership, audio, accessibility, packaging, or release defect; locate the first failed invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in owned layered rendering and asset pipeline and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish display and off-screen surfaces, pixel formats, per-surface and per-pixel alpha, color keys, locks, and conversion timing.
Define clear, background, world, effects, HUD, focus, diagnostics, and presentation order once per frame.
Rotate and scale from an immutable source, recenter output bounds, cache justified variants, and avoid cumulative degradation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Order the regression proof · Debug Render Alternatives
steps.instruction:
Order the Render Alternatives Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python player-input-to-release evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Order the regression proof · Debug Render Alternatives.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Render Alternatives assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Order the regression proof · Debug Render Alternatives, keep Render Alternatives connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.text: A debug Asteroids team handles case 7295 during a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling. Build deterministic Python evidence to compose draw order, surfaces, alpha, asset conversion, transforms, and one explicit frame presentation; retain player goal, revision, Python, pygame-ce, SDL, fixture, seed, input trace, viewport, and result identity; then cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Make ship, hazard, projectile, focus, damage, and status distinguishable without color alone and under reduced effects.

content.value: pygame-ce Surface, Draw, Transform, and Image — Surface formats, alpha, conversion, blitting, locks, drawing, image loading, transformations, and resource behavior.

hints: For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Render Alternatives · fault isolation · Debug Render Alternatives, name the player, revision, runtime, seed, input, time, coordinate, state, entity, collision, render, cue, accessibility, package, release, and desktop-transfer boundary, Render Alternatives precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Render Alternatives · fault isolation · Debug Render Alternatives, trace this concrete input and predict event, intent, simulation, collision, cleanup, rendering, cue, accessibility, failure, pause, rollback, recovery, and desktop evidence before reviewing it: A debug Asteroids team handles case 5726 during a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial. Build deterministic Python evidence to compose draw order, surfaces, alpha, asset conversion, transforms, and one explicit frame presentation; keep every critical cue available through more than color or sound and every action remappable without timing precision; then cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Make ship, hazard, projectile, focus, damage, and status distinguishable without color alone and under reduced effects.

For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Render Alternatives · fault isolation · Debug Render Alternatives, challenge the documented misconception with changed-frame-rate, stall, seam, input, device, collision, cue, accessibility, save, package, failure, recovery, and controlled desktop-transfer evidence: A neon palette is readable and accessible by default.

Another successful run of the same happy-path example. That claim does not survive the render alternatives · fault isolation evidence boundary.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Render Alternatives
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: asteroids-render-alternatives-1lbvrf" that uses case 8256 to compose draw order, surfaces, alpha, asset conversion, transforms, and one explicit frame presentation. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native pygame-ce, SDL, window, controller, mixer, filesystem, package, GPU, installer, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, released pygame-ce 2.5.7, fixed and headless SDL fixtures, real devices, licensed assets, accessibility playtests, profiling, clean installations, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Render Alternatives.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Render Alternatives into observable performance evidence. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Render Alternatives, keep Render Alternatives connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-render-alternatives-1lbvrf
# Competency: asteroids-render-alternatives.
# Case 8256: a controller-remapping accessibility review.
# Operating constraint: test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases.
# Changed case: run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save.
def evidence_asteroids_render_alternatives_1lbvrf(logical_draws: int = 14, presented_frames: int = 14, alpha_assets: int = 4, converted_assets: int = 4, transform_from_original: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1lbvrf = "1lbvrf-frvbl1-8256"
    assert evidence_variant_1lbvrf
    evidence_axes_1lbvrf = (
        "player1lbvrf",
        "seed8256",
        "inputfrvbl1",
        "frame1lbvrf8256",
        "state82561lbvrf",
        "playtestfrvbl18256",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1lbvrf)) == 6
    if logical_draws < 1 or alpha_assets < 0:
        return False, logical_draws, "render-shape"
    if presented_frames != logical_draws:
        return False, presented_frames, "presentation-drift"
    if converted_assets != alpha_assets:
        return False, converted_assets, "surface-format"
    if not transform_from_original:
        return False, alpha_assets, "destructive-transform"
    assert presented_frames == logical_draws
    return True, converted_assets, "owned-render-pipeline"

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Make ship, hazard, projectile, focus, damage, and status distinguishable without color alone and under reduced effects.
